relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job lets the complaint speak from the bitterness of his soul.
He asks the Divine not to condemn him without showing the charge.
He questions whether the Maker would reject the work of his own hands.
He asks whether God sees with human eyes.
He asks whether divine time is small like human time.
Why search him as though hunting guilt?
He insists God knows he is not wicked, yet no one can rescue from that hand.
The hands that shaped him now seem to threaten him.
He asks the Maker to remember the clay before returning it to dust.
He remembers his hidden formation in the body.
Skin, flesh, bones, and sinews were woven around him.
Life and covenant-kindness once guarded his spirit.
Yet he senses a hidden purpose stored in the divine heart.
If he sinned, he feels watched without release.
Whether wicked or righteous, shame and affliction seem to cover him.
If he lifts his head, he feels hunted again.
Witnesses and vexation seem renewed against him.
He asks again why he was brought from the womb.
He imagines never having been seen at all.
He asks for a little brightness before the end of his days.
He sees himself going toward darkness and deep shadow.
The place ahead feels like disorder where even light is dark.
